reviews.review_title: The best VR game I’ve ever played.
reviews.review_description: This is the most fun I’ve ever had playing a VR game. The community is small, but dedicated. This is how to properly make a VR fighting game. I love how your abilities are activated by using stances and not by pressing buttons. The 1:1 movement on this game makes you truly feel like you are the Barbarian, the Samurai, the Pirate, etc. If any game deserves 5 stars, it’s this one.

This game may not have a lot of people on frequently throughout the day BUT during sometimes a lot of people are on so don't listen to these bad reviews the fighting is amazing and skills are easy to learn and overall the community is amazing. This is in my opinion the best sword fighting game in vr.
I don't like it i want my money back it's not good and Noone even plays it anymore pls give my 20 dollars back I WANT A REFUND!!

This game is good if your looking for a non physics game and just wanna swing a sword the physics are pretty cartoonish the items have no weight so it’s pretty easy not much of a workout like other games like this. The idea is there not really any bugs or glitches easy to learn pretty fun tutorial. The only thing I don’t like is the multiplayer it’s like every game is a six year old kid flicking their sword which takes no skill and they just want to win. But overall the different classes are good and fun well thought out the single player is fun and that’s it my honest thought on the game. Like if this helped so others see.

reviews.review_title: Could have been a peak game
reviews.review_description: They quit updating :(

reviews.review_title: This is the Most Accurate Review
reviews.review_description: This game is a very interesting take on vr fighting games. The fighting is based on fencing, and whenever a blade/character gets hit a new round starts where you have another chance to kill the opponent. Each character requires a certain move to make your attacks do more damage. This makes opportunities for some cool a*s combos. Each character can be mastered in different ways. For example, my favorite is the samurai. I love the way you must swing the sword, which is in short swings into poses. All of the moves made to make the samurai’s blade stronger make you feel super bada*s. The fighting is very satisfying and about making the best decisions to win the match. Each character is fun to play, and have their own skillsets that make them all different. I have completed the story mode fully and can say that it was a fun and challenging experience. My overall take from this game is that anyone will have fun playing this masterpiece. The graphics are amazing as well. This game is an easy steal for its price and it is a must have for your library.

Don't buy this fuh ah game, if you want to play against anyone it's anyone that hides behind every little weapon besides their main and the play like scared ah pu$$y and don't get me started a the knight it IS the worst class because it's to op everyone who plays this is just hiding and when you get every item they have your weapon is broken and you have no skills and if you do you weapon is still BROKEN and who ever says that I'm crying or I'm just bad your the one who plays like this so just shush, this game is Butt and you shouldn't buy it and I'm so mad I can't give it a zero and a refund.
I went to the multiplayer tab and there's 6 people playing. 6. not worth the purchase

This game would be a 5 star but for now its 1 star due to me not being able to play.

developer_response.body:
Hi, rufus555

We're so sorry to see you've been having this issue when playing Broken Edge. Please contact us at support@trebuchet.fun so we can help. The last update should have resolved this issue, but if you're still not able to play, contact us!

reviews.review_description:
This game had me playing for hours and got me too really learn how to use each character and has an easy to learn hard to master seamless gameplay 

although to any devs reading it making free movement maybe with a circle boundary to keep it like fencing still but not just forcing one player to move slowly towards the other

reviews.review_title: Great game but it’s like a demo
reviews.review_description: Can you please fix this? I’ve been trying to play broken edge but every time I finish the tutorial I’m stuck with no option to continue. A lot of people are also having this problem. I don’t normally spend money on Vr games but I bought this because this looked really fun. I spent money on the game so I don’t want it to go to waste.

not to mention the several features that didn’t work until the second or third time booting up the game, like progressing after beating the first enemy in solo. the poses for certain moves are very lenient, and others so precise that the moment you try to hit with edge breaker, it thinks you’ve dropped the pose and turns it off. not to mention the fact that (unless this was just another feature not functioning right) the multiplayer matches FORCE you to use codes, so you can only play if you coordinate it with someone outside the game, and with the fact that you can’t just drop into multiplayer, you can only do solo for each archetype, then nothing. This game seems like it was made by passionate people, and has many good things about it, but honestly if i could refund it i would.

Unlike most Vr PvP games were you just Swing your sword like a madman. Broken Edge￼ dose it right by have there PvP Mechanic￼ is not based off How many hits you can do it in a second but for you to fight like how your Character would￼. I think they’re like 8 characters in the game right now￼ and all of them have Unique fighting styles based off of where they come from in real life￼. For instance the Tyrant￼ has a very good longsword and because if it’s Length if you were to wheeled this in real life it Would be heavy so you’d have to move slower￼ and when you play as him you you have to (if you want hung Damage) move Slowly￼ and Wind up in attack￼ (Also here’s a￼ tip, MOVE SLOWLY and use both hands on the blade) (You also unlock the Tyron by beating the first story/quest).

Love the idea and the exceution of it the graphics are sick cool classes and swords but there is no skill based mathmaking in ranked so i just got matched agianst #4 best player 3 times in a row i didnt lose elo bc of the skill diffrnce but it wasnt fun ti play agianst them

This game barley updates and they have incredibly bad hit boxes that are not like just big squares Instead of matching the actual thing, this game is not worth playing as all it is, is bad match making with propers ranks, and hit boxes that will never match up correctly in a million years

developer_response.body:
Hi DAKittyCat,

Your issue sounds more like ping issues than bad hitboxes. When the connection lags, hits can look off even if everything’s working fine on our side. We recommend checking your internet or trying again later to see if it feels smoother.

For matchmaking, the game always tries to find someone close to your rank first, but if no one’s available, it’ll pair you with whoever is around so you don’t end up waiting forever.

We hope you can give Broken Edge another chance in the future!

This is by far ￼THE best VR game I’ve ever played I love the art of the game I found myself playing this for hours then attempting to master blade arts just keep really makes me feel like you’re in a Demon slayer franchise I LOVE this game it’s sooo good I love it only downside is you can’t change how big your play area is￼ I found myself stepping out of it multiple times and everything starts going black if anyone knows how to change this please DM me. ￼

developer_response.body:
Hi JIANM,

Thanks for the kind words! We made it so that all players now have the same playspace because it was unfair for players with smaller areas to compete against those with larger ones. This change helps keep matches as fair and balanced as possible.

We recommend adjusting your guardian so it fits within the game’s playspace.

See you in the arena! ⚔️

Reach ends up being absolutely everything in this game too (since its kinda similar to fencing) so there is an adjectively best class aswell (the massive sword that belongs to a random class they shoved in because they ran out of ideas, thats literally based on nothing)

developer_response.body:
Hi Sorrann,

Thanks for the feedback and sorry to hear the experience felt frustrating! What you’re describing sounds like it could be a mix of connection quality and class matchups. High ping can make timing more difficult, especially in a game where precision matters.

It’s true that some classes have longer reach, but where and how you strike can make a big difference. Hitting near the base of your opponent’s blade can break it faster, which helps balance things out. Starting with a class like the Barbarian can also make things a bit easier than, say, Samurai.

We hope you’ll give Broken Edge another shot! ⚔️

reviews.review_title: Excellent game, would love more like it
reviews.review_description:
One of my favorite games to play recently. I’ll hop on and play my usual fare but this is quickly becoming a staple for VR nights. 

Very fun game, high skill ceiling. Don’t let the intro bots dissuade you, one level doesn’t allow insta kills and that threw me off for a minute. Overall it’s as well done as you could expect when having a bunch of different swords that can’t realistically emulate weight differences.
The barbarian is hard

developer_response.body:
Hi PT_Gamer15,

Thanks for the feedback! The Barbarian can feel tricky at first. His big sword is powerful but slower, so timing your strikes and blocks matters a lot more than with faster classes.

A good tip is to aim for solid, strong hits near the base of your blade. That’s where you’ll deal the most damage. Try to stay calm and let your opponent swing first so you can parry and counter.

You’ve got this! ⚔️

developer_response.body:
Hi RunningWren3721, 

Thanks for the feedback and sorry to hear the difficulty curve felt frustrating! What you’re describing sounds like it could be a mix of class matchups and timing. Some opponents do have longer reach, but the key is in where and how you strike. Hitting the base of their blade with force can break it much faster. Therefore you could use a long sword class like the Barbarian to go thought the solo mode. It's going to be a bit easier than with let's say, the Samurai. Timing your parries and aiming for solid blade contact makes a huge difference.

If you're up for it, jump into our Discord (discord.gg/brokenedge) we’ve got players and devs who’d be happy to share techniques or even spar a bit to help out. You’ve already made it past the first few fights, so you’re definitely on the right track!

We hope you can give Broken Edge a second chance in the future! ⚔️

reviews.review_title: Would love it but it limits just sucks
reviews.review_description: The game looks like it’s a lot of fun but it sucks that you’re movement is limited to your play area
Fix the calibration.

If you want gratuitous murder, this game is not for you. For those who love fencing, HEMA, or even lightsaber show sparring: this game is perfect. It's tactical, classes make sense and embody the sword style they're meant to portray, and as long as controllers stay tracking the balance is nearly perfect between styles.

Theres some insane customization potential available with cosmetics and player class style if you were to let us choose a main weapon and a couple sidearms to mix and match for custom class builds.
It'd make it easier to add new equipment later as well without struggling to tie an entire class style down to a balanced set of weapons.
Ill happily pay microtransactions to look pretty in a game i already bought.
First level is rigged the enemy does like 100 damage compared to me with 8 damage

developer_response.body:
Hi astroastral.VR,

Thanks for sharing your feedback and sorry to hear the first fight felt unfair! The early opponents are meant to teach mechanics, but if it feels like they’re doing way more damage, it might be due to where the hits land. Strikes near the tip of the blade deal less damage, while solid hits closer to the base deal more.

Try focusing on strong, clean hits and blocking early. That can make a big difference in how the damage plays out.

We hope you’ll give it another go and land those big hits! ⚔️

reviews.review_description:
I would prefer a physics based sword game to this.

Update: My preferences of physics in VR games is just my personal opinion. If you don't care for physics I definitely think it's worth a try, especially for MQ+ subscribers. I do think it's interesting it's just not really my style. Thanks devs for a response!

developer_response.body:
Hi HardRockGamer.2004,

Totally fair take. Broken Edge leans more into stylized, tactical swordplay than full physics simulation. The scoring is based on blade control and outplaying your opponent in timing and precision, which can feel pretty different if you’re expecting a physics-heavy experience.

That said, we’re always open to hearing how the scoring could feel more intuitive. If you’ve got specific thoughts, we’d love to hear them on Discord: discord.gg/brokenedge

Thanks for giving it a try and ww're hoping you can give use a second chance in the future! ⚔️

The game was fun but now every time I go in it makes me to tall with the calibration and it makes it unplayable for some characters and it's not my boundary so please fix

developer_response.body:
Hi MyGamesRules,

Thanks for flagging this. That definitely sounds frustrating. The height calibration should adjust properly, but if you're being set too tall despite correct boundaries, it might be a bug, a tracking hiccup or an issue with your boundaries/guardians in your Meta Quest headset.

We’d love to help troubleshoot it and get you back in the fight. Could you drop us a bug report in our Discord with your headset model and maybe make a video of the issue? That’ll help us pin it down: discord.gg/brokenedge

Appreciate you sticking with us! ⚔️

reviews.review_title: Best community and most fun game
reviews.review_description: The devs are always on a constant listening to the community which is amazing, even when something is messed up with the game they assure they'll try their best to get it as quick as possible with their small team. The game is also a really fun sword fighting game that can be both casually or competively played
This game is top notch, its well-polished and well-designed.
Only played a bit but for the money I bought it was worth it, game has amazing visuals, different ways to play, and many things to master. Though keep in mind IT IS NOT physics based
Its so good second best sword fight game and i have a suggesties maybe you can add a assasin class

If your looking for a physics based pvp this isn’t it but if you can learn to appreciate the gameplay for what it’s meant to withhold I think you’ll find yourself pleasantly surprised with this game it has very enjoyable strategy based melee combat that gets me to keep putting on my oculus over and over 7.5/10

Game is amazing, 10/10 but I feel like there should be some upgrades or customization to the characters. For example how about replace one on the samurais wakizashi with a kanabo. Or change the colors of the characters.

this game is one of the most anointing games in terms of classes just because most of the regular classes are normal but the long sword is so broken that i can play this game without getting bored of long spammers i will change my review to 5 stars in a second if they can fix this problem. ￼

reviews.review_description: Despite some rough edges during the basic tutorial, the game is a masterpiece in depth and mechanics. The satisfaction factor is here and pvp duels feel fair. You should try it yourself! Highly recommended!
It’s so f$&k$&g annoying when I hit the second wind pose perfectly and it won’t f$(k@:g work but besides that and it’s sh$tt@y gameplay it’s decent

developer_response.body:
Hi DavidRubix,

Sorry the Second Wind mechanic has been giving you such a rough time, we know how frustrating it can be when you feel like you’re doing it right and it still doesn’t register. It relies on precise positioning and timing, but if it’s consistently failing for you, that might point to a bug or calibration issue.

We’d love to look into it! If you’re willing, drop us a quick message in our Discord with what platform you’re on and when it tends to happen: discord.gg/brokenedge. Both devs and players there are super helpful.

Appreciate you sticking with it even with the rough spots. We’re listening and always working to improve! ⚔️

reviews.review_description: Literally one of the best VR games I’ve ever played my friend gifted me this and I have been playing it since that this game is so freaking fun, stop reading reviews and just get it already
A lot of people give up on the game right when they start because it’s a little bit confusing to learn and there’s a high skill curve, but it’s actually really fun and amazing way to stay active ￼
No chance against the first enemy, bad tutorial

developer_response.body:
Hi SpikyPanda3340,

Sorry to hear that! The first fight can feel rough if the tutorial doesn’t fully click. Broken Edge is all about timing and precision, which isn’t always obvious right away.

That said, you’re not alone, we’ve got a great community on Discord where both devs and experienced players are happy to help out: discord.gg/brokenedge ⚔️

There are also tons of guides and tutorials out there on YouTube and TikTok if you’re looking to improve quickly. Don’t give up, once it clicks, it’s super satisfying!

reviews.review_description:
The idea is to strike away more of your opponents sword than they do of yours, but it seems flawed and removes most of my sword for no reason. This made for a frustrating experience, two stars for nice visuals.

The tutorial is also annoying because you can't skip once you've read the sign, and it waits 30 seconds for you to read two lines.

developer_response.body:
Hi PeteBadger,

Thanks for the honest feedback, we really appreciate it. The sword-breaking system relies on both where and how you strike: clean hits closer to the hilt are much more effective, while glancing blows near the tip do little damage (and can make your own blade break more). It can definitely feel punishing at first, but there’s a lot of depth once the mechanics click.

And totally hear you on the tutorial pacing. We’ve taken notes on that and are looking into ways to streamline it in future updates.

If you’re open to it, swing by our Discord, we’d be happy to help you sharpen your edge! ⚔️ discord.gg/brokenedge

reviews.review_description: Just flat out a good game , steep learning curve especially in higher ranks but still fun either way.
This game has ranked move and an a mode that can be played offline very good for 15 dollars would buy again also discourages spamming
When more patches arrive this review goes to 5 stars!
